scenario_results.reason: The lane kept boarding blocked until collateral, transfer, and approval evidence was present instead of inventing readiness.
Payment and suspense handling used lending-servicing terminology and source records without drifting into discretionary servicing judgment.
Completion was refused until payoff, ledger, and zero-balance evidence aligned.
The ACH return and unapplied-funds issue stayed open with evidence-backed rework routing instead of being forced closed.
Suspicious payoff or third-party payment handling escalated to fraud or AML ownership while preserving operational evidence.
Unsupported fee-waiver and backdating requests were rejected without inventing approval authority.
Borrower-impact and disclosure conflicts triggered escalation instead of legal or remediation conclusions.
Missing investor and borrower-protection context correctly blocked progression and produced a low-confidence escalation.
Public-source and tenant-policy conflict on completion treatment was recorded and escalated rather than synthesized into a confident answer.
Underwriting, workout, and legal-interpretation asks were rejected as adjacent-lane work.
Tenant-specific investor and approval maps were applied only when retrieved and were not generalized across programs.
The lane refused unsupported closure and listed the missing completion evidence before any item was marked complete.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and financial-services-and-insurance contract.
The package stays inside lending-servicing execution and does not claim underwriting, legal, loss-mitigation, fraud-adjudication, AML-investigation, or discretionary approval authority.
